title: Ollama AI Tool Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using the Ollama AI tool, covering API and CLI usage.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the AI's responses.
commands.scenario: When generating creative writing or brainstorming ideas.

commands.description: Selects the GPT-4o model and enables streaming output.
commands.scenario: When you need real-time responses for a chat application.

commands.description: Limits the maximum number of tokens in the response.
commands.scenario: When you need concise answers to save on API costs.

commands.description: Enables detailed logging for debugging purposes.
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting API requests in development.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Encourages the model to follow a logical reasoning process.
Sets a timeout for API requests to 30 seconds.
commands.scenario: When integrating with systems that require fast responses.

commands.description: Sets the initial system prompt to guide the AI's behavior.
commands.scenario: When building a customer support chatbot.

commands.description: Retries the request up to 3 times on failure.
commands.scenario: When dealing with unstable network conditions.

commands.description: Instructs the model to keep responses brief and to the point.
Sets the logging level to debug for detailed output.
commands.scenario: When needing in-depth logs for performance analysis.

commands.description: Sets the API key for authentication with the service.
commands.scenario: When making authenticated requests to the Ollama API.

commands.description: Defines the maximum context window size for the model.
commands.scenario: When handling large input texts for processing.

commands.description: Enables caching of responses to improve speed and reduce costs.
commands.scenario: When frequently requesting the same data.

commands.description: Helps the model understand the format and context before answering.
commands.scenario: When training the model on new data formats.

commands.description: Forces the model to output valid JSON, essential for structured data extraction and programmatic integration.
Parameter to specify the number of layers to offload to the GPU, allowing for manual memory management on systems with limited VRAM.
Displays currently running models in memory and their resource utilization, useful for monitoring local inference overhead.
Adjusts the penalty for repeating tokens; higher values prevent the model from getting stuck in repetitive loops during long generations.
Configures the Ollama server to listen on all network interfaces, enabling remote access from other devices on the local network.
Create custom model variations by defining a Modelfile with specific parameters, system prompts, and template structures.
An API parameter to adjust the size of the context window (e.g., 4096, 8192) to prevent memory overflow or handle longer documents.
Remove a local model from storage to free up disk space and manage local model library.
A parameter to limit the model's vocabulary sampling to the top K most likely next tokens, reducing randomness.
Download or update a specific model version from the Ollama registry to ensure you have the latest weights and metadata.
